name: Lezgian
notes: Lezgian (лезги чӏал) of Dagestan and Azerbaijan, written in a Cyrillic alphabet since 1938 (41 letters). INPUT CONTRACT: Cyrillic orthography, Unicode NFC, lowercased; the palochka must be U+04CF ⟨ӏ⟩ / U+04C0 ⟨Ӏ⟩ — the Latin 'I'/'l'/'1' shapes seen in some printed fonts are not accepted as input, because no consulted source documents them as a spelling convention. Multigraphs carry the ejective, uvular and front-vowel series — кӏ пӏ тӏ цӏ чӏ (ejectives), къ /q/, кь /qʼ/, хъ /qʰ/, гъ /ʁ/, гь /h/, хь /x/, уь /y/ — and are matched by maximal munch, so they win over their component letters; ь never occurs alone. Aspiration is phonemic but NOT written, so к п т ц ч are given aspirated-first with the unaspirated variant as a second candidate; this ambiguity cannot be resolved from the orthography alone. Щ occurs only in Russian loanwords and is LEFT OUT: no source consulted assigns it a Lezgian IPA value. Labialised consonants (a large part of the 54-consonant inventory) are likewise not distinguished in the orthography and are not modelled. Е is given as [e] first (its value after a consonant) with [je] as the second candidate (word-initial / post-vocalic), since the source lists both without positional detail.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Lezgin alphabets — comparative table of Arabic / Latin / Cyrillic / IPA
sources.notes: Letter-by-letter Cyrillic→IPA table actually read (wikitext via action=raw); also the notes that ы = [ə], ё occurs only in ёъ [joʔ] and щ only in Russian loans.

sources.author: Haspelmath, Martin
sources.title: A Grammar of Lezgian
sources.notes: Standard reference grammar; the four-way stop contrast (voiced / aspirated / unaspirated / ejective) and the fact that aspiration is unwritten. Full text not accessible online; used as background, all grapheme values here come from the read Wikipedia tables.

ancestors.notes: Nakh-Daghestanian — the classification node this language sits under.
